SPR-095.goal:
Range Lab's per-street range evolution becomes fast enough to ship,
and the numbers it surfaces are provably consistent with the Line
Study (LSW) lessons that cross-link to it.

WS-205 adds a Float64Array per-combo equity cache computed at
flop-paint-finalize so turn/river narrowing is a filter+weight
operation (~10ms/turn-card) rather than a full per-frame recompute.
WS-206 locks theoretical consistency: a CI parity test asserts LSW's
asserted equity == RL's computed equity within tolerance, so an engine
change can't silently break authored drill content.

SPR-096.title: LSW postflop content correctness — impossible-combo fix + turn bucket-shift nodes
SPR-096.goal:
The drill-line content the new LSW↔RL parity invariant audits is
theory-clean: no impossible holdings remain, and turn nodes where a
hand's strength bucket shifts are authored from a verified node list
rather than an estimate.

SPR-097.title: Range Lab Gate 3 (Research) — author RL JTBDs + advance to design-ready
SPR-097.goal:
Range Lab's JTBD research is authored and the project advances to
design-ready — the last design blocker before the Gate 4 surface spec
(WS-055) that unblocks Phase 1-2 implementation.

SPR-098.title: Range Lab Gate 4 (Design) — Range Lab capability section in postflop-drills surface
SPR-098.goal:
Range Lab is design-complete — the postflop-drills surface carries a full
Range Lab capability spec (paint UX, filters, histogram, comparison,
per-street evolution, validate, cross-links, mobile decision) so Phase 1-2
engineering (WS-056/057) can implement against it.

SPR-099.title: Range Lab Phase 1 — interactive range paint + turn/river in Explorer mode
SPR-099.goal:
Range Lab becomes usable for the first time — you can paint a custom hand
range, run the board out to the river, and have the painted range persist
for the session.

SPR-100.title: Range Lab Phase 2 — equity histogram + subrange filters (scope of 'Flopzilla parity')
SPR-100.goal:
Range Lab becomes an interactive equity-analysis tool — paint a range, read
its equity distribution against a board, and filter to subranges — the first
analysis layer on top of the paint matrix shipped in SPR-099.

SPR-101.title: Range Lab Phase 2b — range-vs-range comparison (paint a 2nd range)
SPR-101.goal:
Range Lab reaches full Flopzilla parity — paint two ranges and compare their
equity and hand-type makeup side by side on a board. Cashes the third feature
deferred from Phase 2 (SPR-100).

SPR-102.title: HSP-A3 — Turn archetype taxonomy + templates
SPR-102.goal:
Hero State coaching narratives extend to the turn — when reviewing a hand,
the coach can describe turn decisions (double-barrels, probes, check-raises)
the same way it already handles preflop and flop. Unblocks WS-153 (classifier
turn/river extension), which lets Self-Coach leak rules cover later streets.

SPR-103.title: HSP v2 turn+river close-out — river archetypes + classifier extension
SPR-103.goal:
Hero State archetypes work on the turn and river — not just preflop and flop.
classifyArchetype() returns a real archetype for every street instead of
throwing NotImplementedError. Closes the v2 "archetypes on every street"
milestone end-to-end: river templates (WS-152) → classifier wired for
turn+river (WS-153). Unblocks downstream Self-Coach leak rules and any
HeroState consumer (HandReplay, SCF) on later streets.

SPR-104.title: HSP — Orchestrator deriveActionContext() turn/river precision (Path A: heroState-local)
SPR-104.goal:
Turn/river HSP archetype routing becomes precise from action-history alone.
Callers (HandReplay, SCF, future live coach) no longer have to pass
gameState.actionContext explicitly for buildHeroState() to route to the right
WS-153 archetype — buildHeroState.deriveActionContext() reconstructs
BARREL / VS_BARREL / PROBE / CBET (delayed) / VS_DONK from gameState
.actionSequence per street, and optionally derives sizingFraction from
pendingBetSize / pot for RIVER_BLOCK_BET / RIVER_VS_BLOCK_BET routing.

SPR-105.title: HSP-X1 — Adjustment composition rule (resolves HERO_STATE_DESIGN.md §7.2)
SPR-105.goal:
HSP v2 adjustment composition lands: when multiple villain tendencies fire on
the same hero decision (e.g., calling-station AND short-stacked), the final
delta is computed by explicit per-axis rules grounded in poker theory — not
serial application order. Resolves the last open §7.2 question in
HERO_STATE_DESIGN.md and unblocks WS-150's HSP v2 milestone.

SPR-106.title: HSP-M1 — Full multiway model (HSP v2 close-out — lone hard-dep remaining)
SPR-106.goal:
Hero State can reason about 3+ way pots. Author the multiway archetype family
+ sequential-decision modeling + first-principles bluff-frequency adjustment
that replaces the FLOP_MULTIWAY v1 stub. Resolves HERO_STATE_DESIGN.md §7.4
and collapses HSP v2 milestone to the two conditional tail items (WS-156
caching + WS-157 LLM authoring).

SPR-107.title: Mid-hand tag-for-review — one-tap flag + Review Queue (WS-190)
SPR-107.goal:
You can flag a hand for review with one tap mid-session, then find all your
flagged hands later in a Review Queue — turning "I should think about that
hand" into a durable, studyable list instead of relying on memory.

SPR-108.title: SCF leak rules — ship 2 more catalog rules incl. first multiway-specific (WS-146 5th claim)
SPR-108.goal:
The self-coach catches two more real mistakes at the table — including its
first multiway-specific leak (over-bluffing in 3+ way pots) — so it covers a
wider slice of the spots that actually cost you money.

SPR-109.title: SCF leak rules — frequency-of-aggression follow-through (turn barrel + RFI open-fold) (WS-146 6th claim)
SPR-109.goal:
The self-coach catches two more real mistakes — barreling the turn too often,
and opening too tight preflop — by reusing the aggression-frequency machinery
we built last sprint, with no new plumbing.

SPR-110.title: PIO recognition scoring + confidence — unblocked core (WS-164)
SPR-110.goal:
Looking up a known player by their physical features returns a ranked,
confidence-scored shortlist — so recognition at the table is fast and the
top match is trustworthy.
